reason: Both answers correctly identify the §412 three-month grace window, that registration is required before filing suit (citing Fourth Estate), and that infringement predating registration limits recovery to actual damages/profits, while suggesting DMCA takedowns as an immediate no-registration remedy. They reach the same actionable conclusion via essentially the same reasoning. The only substantive difference is the continuing-infringement nuance: A frames it as a genuine circuit split ('courts split on this... don't count on it'), while B asserts courts 'generally' treat it as a continuation (also concluding not to rely on it). Both hedges are defensible characterizations of the case law and neither materially changes what the asker should do. B's added 'consult IP counsel' caveat is boilerplate that doesn't add new decision-relevant information. Overall the two answers are comparable in correctness, coverage, and actionability with no padding advantage either way.
